title: Workflow Education + AAR Archive + State Graph Redundancy

missions.title: Acknowledge + apply updated workflow conventions
deliverables: 3-destination storage protocol confirmed: Context Vault + Local Attachments + Google Drive
Retroactive ID convention adopted: RETRO-YYYYMMDD-XX for pre-versioning sessions

missions.title: Organize all existing AARs + create AAR_CATALOG.json
deliverables: context_vault/aar/AAR_CATALOG.json — 24 AARs cataloged with retroactive IDs
All entries include: aar_id, filename, vault_path, date, phase, directive, format, retroactive, status
21 entries confirmed present in vault at time of catalog creation

missions.title: Create evolution_archive.json + EVOLUTION_INDEX.md
deliverables: context_vault/EVOLUTION_INDEX.md — human-readable entry point, phase timeline table, capability growth chart, architectural decisions, market triggers, successor directives

missions.title: Build state_redundancy.py + wire into OrganismState
deliverables: epos/state/universal_state_graph.py updated — update() now calls snapshot_state() after every write
snapshot_state(): timestamped snapshots with SHA-256 checksums, prunes to 30 retained
validate_state(): compares current file checksum against most recent snapshot
recover_state(): iterates newest→oldest, restores first valid snapshot
list_snapshots() + health_check() — utilities and Doctor-compatible health check
Event: system.state.snapshot_created published on every snapshot

capabilities_gained: AAR_CATALOG.json — 24 AARs with retroactive IDs, full chain of custody
evolution_archive.json — complete evolutionary DNA record from Genesis (2026-03-27) to present
EVOLUTION_INDEX.md — human-readable institutional memory entry point
Doctor-compatible health_check() for state redundancy layer

architectural_decisions.decision: Internal secrets auto-generated, external secrets human-gated
architectural_decisions.rationale: EPOS can rotate its own secrets; cannot invent external API keys

architectural_decisions.decision: Directive ID in every file header
architectural_decisions.rationale: Any file can be traced to the session that created it

lessons_learned: Module-level singleton locks (class-level threading.Lock) can cause false negatives when multiple imports share state in a single Python process — always isolate verification checks
SHA-256 checksum of raw file bytes differs from checksum of re-serialized JSON — recovery correctly trusts snapshot JSON rather than byte-matching
Lazy import pattern (try/except inside method) is the correct pattern for optional dependencies — non-fatal failure preserves core functionality

wire_to_doctor.note: health_check() is Doctor-compatible. Wire validate_state() as Doctor check in epos_doctor.py on next directive that touches the Doctor node.

signatures.executing_agent: Claude Code (bootstrap) — final session before full SCC handoff
signatures.constitutional_gate: EPOS Constitution v3.1 — AAR is constitutional mandate, not optional
